reviews.title: Excellent Dive Computer and Smart Watch
reviews.text:
I have been wearing this for a solid 4 months, using it to track dives, swimming, running, walking, treadmill workouts, etc. In watch mode the battery lasts a long time, but for activities you will find yourself charging it after about 5-8 hours of activity use.

I was a bit worried my skin would react as I cannot usually wear anything metal unless high quality gold or sterling silver. No problems with this watch - the band is comfortable and the watch, while a bit larger than I was used to, fits well and without any reaction.

Functions while diving were flawless. You have a lot of parameter's you can set - this thing is designed for divers. It tracks entry and exit locations accurately. I wish I could tell it when my dive is starting and when done, the automatic feature is a bit annoying in this regard. However, it is one less thing to remember when you are in the water and doing a buddy check to get going. It is pretty neat to how nicely it updates the app on your phone and makes sharing easy.

Pool swimming is not entirely accurate - the watch cannot record kickboard laps, for example, so if you like to vary your workout you won't have accurate times or distances. Basically, any time you are not completing actual strokes you won't get a record.

Treadmill work - you can adjust the settings to match your treadmill readings. I found it was not very accurate and finally quit bothering to adjust it. The watch was either lower in distance or higher every time, but close enough to just let it be. At least once you leave it alone you get consistent numbers, and you can also record numbers from the treadmill if you want.

Walking and trail running features are great - I love seeing where I was with the GPS integration. I work out regularly on a measured trail and the distances are spot-on with the MK2S. There are workouts it will suggest, but I tend to stick to my own and ignore those. I was really frustrated to find my VO2 was consistently "Poor" all fall, but have seen some improvement recently with regular workouts. Kind of cool getting readings like that.

I plan to use this watch for backpacking as well - I have not had an opportunity to test it since the purchase, but my last trip my sister had a different model and it worked well other than eating power. You absolutely have to carry one or two good battery packs to charge it. I really wish this had a solar option, which I understand is in future plans, so I will more than likely upgrade when that is offered. Even so, hiking puts your wrist in the shade a lot of the time unless you are hiking alpine locations, so not sure how well solar would actually work.

All said, I love my "toy". It helps keep me motivated when I see I have been in the office too long or my scores are a lot lower. I am competitive and happy to compete against myself, so it works well. I don't use the connections, although I have considered seeing if anyone I know has a Garmin, but so far its all me :D While pricy, for someone who loves a wide variety of athletic activity, you can't beat the Garmin Mk2 in any of its versions. My husband is still trying to decide if he wants the Mk2S or the Mk2i but he will be getting one or the other. Fantastic product.

reviews.title: Poor Measurements - Support Cannot Help
reviews.text:
After charging for 30 minutes as instructed, I paired the smart watch with the app. This was easy, and the one reason I gave it 2 stars instead of 1. The other reason for 2 stars, the interface was easier to use than I expected.

But if the measurements are not accurate, ease of use doesn't matter.

I bought this smart watch to track my heart rate while exercising—mainly walking. After recent heart procedures, my doctor wants me to keep my pulse below a certain rate. Each time I measure, the pulse differs substantially from the pulse oximeter that always agrees with my doctor's office pulse oximeter, and heart monitor. It goes as high as 90 when the PO says 52. I took multiple measurements, including taking my own pulse. The closest measurements were 60 when the actual rate was 51 or 52.

Support is through a chat on the app. I did not wait long for support, however they did not try to help me. They insisted that the heart rate changes. That I had to let it sync with the app. Not once did they address the possibility that the smart watch might be reading the rate wrong. Told me to try again after I had already tried multiple times.

Focusing on anything but the issue I am reporting does not help me. Even suggesting I exercise and measure my heart rate. Telling me the human heart rate is constantly changing. Literally, answers like they were talking to a small child.

I had high hopes for this device. It disappointed on two levels - Heart Rate, Support. I cannot recommend. Look for a different smart watch if you need accurate heart rate measurements.

reviews.title: NOT AS DESCRIBED AT ALL
reviews.text: I carefully read all reviews before purchasing this smart watch but was so disappointed. It would not hold preferences & kept defaulting to kg & cm. Would not toggle from one thing to another on app. Had to go all the way out of app & go back in before it would toggle from steps to profile to anything else. Sometimes I'd get my cell to ring on my watch, never could answer it or text back from the watch. I'd rather pay the extra & get an apple watch that actually works. I can't tell you how many times I set the preferences & saved them only to find it defaulted right back the next day.  One of the sorriest watches I've ever owned & I kept it 2 weeks before returning it really trying to make it work.

reviews.title: Sleep function is worthless
reviews.text: I really like this watch, has everything I wanted and everything works with one exception:  the sleep analysis feature is worthless.  It only records sleep between 10:00 pm and 8:00 am and if you move or get up it screws up everything. Good customer service, they really try to help, but they will tell you if you get up to go to the bathroom, watch begins recording all over again. Too bad!

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch3 2020 Smartwatch (Bluetooth + Wi-Fi + GPS) International Model (Bronze, 41mm)
description: The only smartwatch with a precise rotating bezel for navigating between apps. Larger screen, but lighter and thinner for more comfort: With its subtle rotating bezel, it's easier to see and focus on what's important on an even larger screen. The Galaxy Watch3 is thinner and smaller, and up to 15% lighter with a sophisticated look that, thanks to its reduced frame, is perfect for the wrist. Breathe easier with built-in oxygen measurement: The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood. Because oxygen saturation is directly related to physical activity, you can use the Galaxy Watch3 to push your routine to the next level. Enjoy a better sleep: The Galaxy Watch3 records your active sleep, deep sleep, and total sleep time to keep track and improve the quality of your sleep. Samsung partnered with the National Sleep Foundation to include educational content and provide tips on your device to help you sleep better. Do your best and then repeat it: With over 120 home workout programs to choose from, the Galaxy Watch3 helps you move your body at your own pace. Pick one from your phone and stream it on your TV. It will show you your heart rate in real-time while you exercise. Use the Ridge Sport strap for even more accurate stats. Continue your conversations: When you receive a new message, the Galaxy Watch3 shows your conversation history so you can follow them. You can also view images without needing an extra touch, making it easier to keep up while you're working out. Add some context with the smart answer: Respond to messages at all times and without missing a beat. The Galaxy Watch3's smart reply analyzes the texts and photos you receive and gives you possible answers in different categories, such as food, celebrations, selfies and much more.

title: Apple Watch SE (GPS, 40mm) - Silver Aluminum Case with Abyss Blue Sport Band (Renewed)
description: Stay safe, active, and healthy with the Apple Watch SE. The SE sports the same bright LTPO OLED Retina display as the Series 6, giving you a bright screen you can view at a glance, even in bright sunlight. On it, you can view all of your fitness metrics, health stats, and mobile notifications. When it comes to health, the SE's optical heart rate monitor can give you indicators of a possible medical condition with notifications for high or low heart rates, as well as irregular rhythms. Even healthy habits are supported, such as hand-washing detection, which displays a 20-second animated countdown as you scrub. Fitness is a large part of staying healthy, and the Apple Watch SE has a variety of ways to assist you in reaching your goals. Activity Rings displays three rings that correspond to movement, exercise, and standing. Stay mobile throughout the day to hit new personal bests as you close the rings.You can also choose from a variety of workouts, such as running, swimming, yoga, cycling, and more; with the watch recording and tracking their specific metrics. Whether inside or outdoors, the SE can guide you via GPS and even provide continuous elevation updates on screen. As you move, choose from a variety of music, podcasts, and audiobooks to entertain and motivate you. For added fun, share your activity rings with friends and challenge them to 7-day activity competitions. Throughout the day, the Apple Watch SE keeps you connected with the world around you. When paired with your iPhone, you can make calls and send texts directly from your wrist, navigate your surroundings with Maps, buy items with Apple Pay, and use your voice to interact with Siri. Made to last in almost any kind of weather, the Apple Watch SE is water-resistant up to 164'. Use its 32GB of built-in storage for apps, watch faces, music, and more.
features: LEAVE YOUR PHONE IN YOUR POCKET: Apple Watch SE GPS Model lets you call, text, and get directions from your wrist, while leaving your phone in your pocket. It offers multiple connectivity options, including: Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, and NFC to suit your needs, whatever they might be.

LARGE RETINA OLED DISPLAY: The SE sports a bright LTPO OLED Retina display, giving you a bright screen you can view at a glance, even in bright sunlight. A variety of watch faces are available for the SE watch, including faces that provide essential information for specific activities.

LOADED WITH FEATURES: When paired with your iPhone, you can make calls and send texts from your wrist, navigate with Maps, buy items with Apple Pay, and use your voice to activate Siri. Made to last in almost any kind of weather, the Apple Watch SE is water-resistant up to 164'.

WORKOUTS THAT DON'T QUIT: Cycling, yoga, swimming, high-intensity interval training...the list goes on. You name it, Apple Watch measures it. Set workout-specific goals, see full summaries when you're done, and track how you're trending over time in the Activity app on your iPhone.

GET LOST IN YOUR MUSIC: With Apple Music on your wrist, you've got 60 million tracks of musical motivation to take you places. You can also catch up on the latest podcasts or listen to an audiobook if that's your thing. Stream everything you need right from your watch, even without your phone.

reviews.title: Great Purchase!

Want to say this is my first time buying an Apple watch, I have transitioned from Samsung to Apple (phone and watch) and again, I made a great choice and purchase. I DO RECOMMEND THIS PRODUCT!

features: [Turn Your Good Intentions into Lifelong Habits] Get motivated and step up your daily activity goals with Octandra Active Fitness Watch for Men and Women! Apart from its main task of monitoring your health and fitness, it works as your personal digital assistant!

[Stay Active with Very Fit Pro] Download VeryFitPro from Google Play or App Store, pair the sports watch and start using its advanced health monitoring tools - Compatible with both Android 4.4, iOS 8.0 or above!

Can be worn while jogging in the rain or doing the dishes) and soft TPE straps which suit wrist sizes from 5.4 to 7.6 inches!

[Convenient USB Charging with Long-Lasting Battery] Enjoy 5-7 days of battery life with 1-2 hours charging time via built-in USB! (During actual use, battery life is related to various factors such as settings, operations, and operating conditions, and therefore may differ from the test conditions)
[A Sweet Life Companion] Never miss your calls, texts, and social app notifications with Octandra Active Exercise Watch!
[More Practical Tools] You can use our running watch as a stop watch, sedentary reminder, alarm watch, and camera shutter remote control of your smartphone!

[Important] This fitness band is not a medical device. All the measurement data and results are provided for reference only and cannot be compared with the results of professional medical devices, used to perform professional diagnosis or monitoring of any medical conditions. Due to external factors (such as arm hair, tattoos, or darker skin), there may be cases where measurement results are inaccurate or even unavailable!

reviews.title: Not a good buy
reviews.text: The 1st one I got "went crazy" and started just cycling thru the various screens on the watch.  I returned it and got a replacement because we all know that "stuff happens".  I got the replacement and it seemed to be working well until day 28, when it stopped connecting with the app at all, even though I installed and uninstalled it several times as the troubleshooting recommended.  Then it started draining my battery dry and that meant it needed to be returned.  Don't waste your money on this one unless you have LOTS of time to fiddle with it!

reviews.title: Good.
reviews.text: Good.

reviews.title: I wanted to like this product...
reviews.text:
I wanted a simple activity tracker and this looked like it was perfect. There are a number of things that worked well, battery life, simplicity, matched my needs... However, the accuracy is non existent. I was at the gym working out vigorously and my heart rate was 50 and dropping. I checked it with another app on my phone and I was approaching 140. When I am at rest it creeps into the 90's. by the way, I have entered all profile info. The steps are also way off as I tested against an app on my phone.

I tried but its not worth the effort.

reviews.title: great looking watch and great for running and biking
reviews.text:
I really like this watch. I replaced and old forerunner. (many generation old) It feels great and looks good since you can download and tailor the watch faces. I have downloaded music to it using MediaMonkey and it worked fine. So far, synching with earbuds Jaybird Tarah Pro and Vistas, has been good. The availability of watch faces and activity widgets is nice.
But
Be warned, it takes a little bit to set it up and understand how to understand how to download and update activity widgets. Updating of watch faces isn't too bad. Be patient and read the user guide, there is a lot of functionality you should be aware of whether you use them or not.

reviews.title: Great purchase
reviews.text:
Great, does all of the stuff running nerds love. Spotify is a hassle to sync when i want to adjust my playlists. This is especially annoying for podcast listeners such as myself. For this reason, I could have gone without the music upgrade and just used my phone but I still use the music feature a good amount for my shorter speed runs where I need the running jams!

But obviously I didn’t take a star because Garmin is the GOAT

reviews.title: Great watch
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband, an avid runner, for our anniversary. He is super into tracking, numbers, stats, etc. And he loves this watch! He previously had the Forerunner 235 Garmin but it was probably close to five years old. He had done alot of research on this one verses the newer models and this one still came out ahead and did not disappoint!

reviews.title: great fitness watch!
reviews.text: if you are looking for a smart watch, then this is not for you. however, if you want a fitness watch, then get this one! the gps tracking is great and accurate! it takes a little bit to get used to the buttons and where all the settings are, but i love that i can customize it to do just what i want in terms of display, audio outputs, etc. the band is comfortable and the watch face is a good size to see my stats while working out without being overly cumbersome (i'm a small 5'0" female). i love that it can play my spotify playlists! the only thing of note is that it will not be able to get any data such as weather, sms notifications, calendar updates, emails, phone calls, or send emergency assistance notices unless you have your phone with you and connected via bluetooth. the point of this watch for me was to keep my phone at home so those features don't work during my run, but it does do the fitness tracking and play previously setup spotify playlists, so it's exactly what i want and need. i don't use it for sleep tracking since i only wear it during workouts. love the heart rate monitoring too!

reviews.title: Sleep Tracking and Incoming Call Notification (on android) are EPIC failures
reviews.text:
Overwhelming 5 start reviews really make me skeptical that the reviews themselves are genuine....
Overall the watch is not bad but really needs functional work. The Android Dev/QA team should have some re-training/structuring after releasing this to the general public...
Sleep tracking shows me as sleeping if I am in bed relaxed watching tv during sleep hours, making it useless. I only receive an incoming call notification on the watch if my phone screen is unlocked...
missed calls come in every time..

no problems with GPS, Battery Life, Music , Bluetooth, feeds to 3rd party athletic tracking apps. all those work fine.

Not sure I would buy this again.

reviews.title: Pretty good
reviews.text:
Pretty good smartwatch. I needed something to replace my Galaxy Active, but didn't want to spend $100+ on something with a horrible battery life. I bought this one and for a while I was disappointed. I had to charge the watch each night and the steps weren't as accurate. I changed the setting so that the screen doesn't turn on when you move your wrist and that has saved the battery big time. I'm on day 4 and only down to 50% which means I'm now able to track sleep as well. I still think the steps aren't as accurate because it almost matches the steps in my phone's health app, but I'm notorious for leaving my phone places. I also miss being able to limit the options for exercise types in the watch face. I have to scroll through a long list each time. Overall, pretty good product for a cheaper price.

Update: I originally gave this 5 stars, but I have to take 2 away. A few days ago, the watch all of a sudden stopped counting steps. For the price, it was perfect until it stopped working. Back to the drawing board.

reviews.title: The perfect watch
reviews.text: Hubby loves his birthday watch , has no complaints. Says it’s the perfect watch, for all he uses it for. .

reviews.title: Includes the Tools and 1 Pair of New Pins
reviews.text: These fit the Garmin 620s perfectly. The tools are very sturdy as I used them to fix two watchbands with nary a scratch. If these fit your watch they will certainly stand you in good stead. Great price for a great product.

reviews.title: Alive colors
reviews.text: Excellent. His colors are as is in person. alive and cute. It fits perfectly on the Forerunner 235.
